title: New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter

description: Indulge in the ultimate American dinner with a perfectly seared New York Strip Steak, rich with garlic herb butter. This recipe delivers a tender, juicy steak with a beautiful crust, making every bite a memorable experience. Elevate your home cooking with this classic steakhouse favorite, guaranteed to impress.

ingredients.notes: or other high smoke point oil like avocado oil
1 stick, cold and cut into cubes

instructions.title: Prepare and Season the Steaks
instructions.text: Remove the New York strip steaks from the refrigerator at least 30 minutes before cooking to allow them to come closer to room temperature. This promotes even cooking. Pat the steaks thoroughly dry with paper towels. Season generously on all sides with kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper.

instructions.title: Preheat the Skillet
instructions.text: Place a heavy-bottomed skillet, preferably cast iron, over high heat. Add the olive oil and heat until it begins to shimmer and lightly smoke. It is crucial to have the pan very hot for a good sear.

instructions.title: Sear the Steaks
instructions.text: Carefully place the seasoned steaks into the hot skillet. Do not overcrowd the pan; cook one or two at a time if necessary. Sear undisturbed for 2-3 minutes per side until a deep, golden-brown crust forms. Use tongs to sear the edges for about 30 seconds each.

instructions.title: Add Garlic Butter and Baste
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to medium. Add the cold unsalted butter, smashed garlic cloves, and fresh rosemary sprigs to the skillet. As the butter melts, tilt the pan slightly and use a large spoon to continuously baste the melted garlic herb butter over the steaks for another 2-3 minutes.

instructions.title: Monitor Temperature for Doneness
instructions.text: Continue basting until the steaks reach your desired internal temperature. For medium-rare, aim for 125-130F; for medium, 130-135F. Remember the temperature will rise a few degrees while resting. For a thicker steak, you can transfer the skillet to a preheated 400F oven after searing for a few minutes to finish cooking.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Transfer the cooked steaks to a cutting board and loosely tent them with foil. Let the steaks rest for at least 10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring a tender and moist steak. Slice against the grain, spoon any remaining garlic butter from the pan over the slices, and serve immediately.

tips: For an authentic steakhouse crust, ensure your cast iron skillet is smoking hot before adding the steak. This creates a beautiful Maillard reaction.
Always use cold butter when basting. The cold butter melts slower, emulsifying with the pan juices and creating a richer, more flavorful basting liquid.
Resting the steak is non-negotiable! It allows the muscle fibers to relax and reabsorb juices, resulting in a significantly more tender and juicy steak.
article:
Mastering a truly exceptional steak at home is a culinary achievement that brings immense satisfaction. Forget the fancy steakhouses; with the right technique and a few quality ingredients, you can create a restaurant-worthy meal in your own kitchen. One of the most beloved and straightforward ways to elevate a prime cut is by infusing it with rich, aromatic garlic butter. This article will guide you through crafting the perfect New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter, a quintessential American dinner recipe that promises a symphony of flavors and a truly unforgettable dining experience.

## What is New York Strip Steak?

The New York Strip, also known as the strip loin, Kansas City strip, or simply the "strip steak," is a highly prized cut of beef renowned for its excellent balance of tenderness, flavor, and texture. It comes from the short loin of the cow, a muscle that does very little work, contributing to its inherent tenderness. Characterized by its fine grain and a good amount of marbling – those delicate streaks of fat that melt during cooking, imparting succulence and flavor – the New York Strip typically has a thick fat cap along one side, which can be trimmed or left on for added flavor and moisture. Its robust beefy taste and satisfying chew make it a favorite among steak enthusiasts and a superb choice for pan-searing, grilling, or broiling.

## Ingredients You'll Need

Creating a magnificent New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter doesn't require an extensive list of ingredients, emphasizing quality over quantity.

For the Steak:
*   2 New York Strip Steaks, 1 to 1.5 inches thick (about 12-16 oz each)
*   1-2 tablespoons high smoke point oil (canola, grapeseed, avocado, or refined olive oil)
*   1-2 teaspoons kosher salt (or sea salt)
*   1 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper

For the Garlic Butter:
*   4 tablespoons unsalted butter
*   4-6 cloves garlic, minced
*   2 sprigs fresh rosemary (or 1 tablespoon chopped fresh thyme)
*   Optional: A pinch of red pepper flakes for a subtle kick

## Step-by-Step Instructions: How to Cook New York Strip Steak with Garlic Butter

Follow these steps precisely for a steakhouse-quality New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter.

1.  **Preparation is Key:**
    *   Remove the New York Strip steaks from the refrigerator at least 30-60 minutes before cooking. This allows them to come closer to room temperature, ensuring more even cooking.
    *   Pat the steaks thoroughly dry with paper towels. Excess moisture on the surface will steam the steak rather than sear it, preventing that desirable crust.
    *   Season generously all over with kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper. Don't be shy; a good amount of seasoning is crucial for flavor.

2.  **Heat the Pan:**
    *   Place a heavy-bottomed skillet (cast iron is ideal for its heat retention) over high heat. Let it preheat for 3-5 minutes until it's smoking hot. This extreme heat is vital for achieving a perfect sear.
    *   Add the high smoke point oil to the hot pan. Swirl to coat the bottom.

3.  **Sear the Steak:**
    *   Carefully place the seasoned steaks into the hot pan. They should sizzle loudly immediately.
    *   Sear for 2-4 minutes per side without moving them. This creates a beautiful, deeply browned crust (the Maillard reaction). For a 1.5-inch thick steak, you might sear for 3 minutes per side.
    *   Use tongs to sear the edges as well, holding the steak on its sides for about 1 minute each, especially if there's a thick fat cap.

4.  **Baste with Garlic Butter:**
    *   Reduce the heat to medium-low.
    *   Add the unsalted butter, minced garlic, and fresh rosemary (or thyme) to the pan. The butter will melt and foam.
    *   Tilt the pan slightly and, using a large spoon, continuously baste the melting garlic butter mixture over the steaks. Do this for 2-4 minutes, flipping the steaks once or twice during the basting process. The garlic and herbs will infuse the butter, which then coats and flavors the steak.

5.  **Check for Doneness:**
    *   The best way to determine doneness is with an instant-read meat thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the steak, avoiding bone or fat.
        *   Rare: 125-130°F (52-54°C)
        *   Medium-Rare: 130-135°F (54-57°C) - *Highly recommended for New York Strip*
        *   Medium: 135-140°F (57-60°C)
        *   Medium-Well: 140-145°F (60-63°C)
    *   Remember, the internal temperature will rise by 5-10 degrees during resting. Remove the steak from the pan when it's about 5 degrees below your desired final temperature.

6.  **Rest the Steak:**
    *   Transfer the cooked steaks to a cutting board. Tent loosely with aluminum foil.
    *   Allow the steaks to rest for 5-10 minutes. This critical step allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, resulting in a more tender and flavorful steak. If you cut it too soon, the juices will run out, leaving you with a dry steak.
    *   After resting, slice the steak against the grain into thick pieces. Spoon any remaining pan juices and garlic butter over the slices.

## Expert Tips for the Perfect Steak

Achieving steak perfection is within reach with these pro tips:

*   **Room Temperature is Crucial:** Cold steaks cook unevenly. Giving them time to warm up ensures consistent cooking from edge to center.
*   **Pat Dry, Always:** Moisture is the enemy of a good sear. Dry steaks brown, wet steaks steam.
*   **Don't Fear the Heat:** A screaming hot pan is essential for developing that coveted crust.
*   **Don't Overcrowd:** If cooking more than two steaks, cook them in batches to maintain high pan temperature. Overcrowding lowers the temperature, leading to steaming instead of searing.
*   **Use a Thermometer:** It's the only foolproof way to achieve your desired doneness consistently. Guessing often leads to overcooked steak.
*   **Resting is Non-Negotiable:** Patience is a virtue here. Resist the urge to cut into it immediately.
*   **Quality Matters:** Start with a good quality New York Strip steak. Grass-fed or prime cuts will offer superior flavor and tenderness.

## Serving Suggestions & Side Dishes

Your magnificent New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter deserves equally delicious accompaniments. Here are some classic and creative ideas:

*   **Classic Comfort:** Creamy mashed potatoes or fluffy baked potatoes, roasted asparagus, sautéed green beans, or creamed spinach.
*   **Fresh & Vibrant:** A crisp Caesar salad, a simple garden salad with a vinaigrette, or grilled seasonal vegetables.
*   **Hearty Options:** Garlic parmesan fries, wild mushroom risotto, or a hearty wedge salad.
*   **Sauces:** While the garlic butter provides ample flavor, a simple pan sauce made with a splash of red wine or beef broth can add another layer of complexity.

## Storage and Reheating Leftovers

While a freshly cooked steak is always best, sometimes you have leftovers.

*   **Storage:** Store any leftover New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days.
*   **Reheating:** To best preserve texture and flavor, avoid the microwave.
    *   **Oven Method:** Slice the steak thinly, place it on a baking sheet, and warm gently in a preheated oven at 250°F (120°C) for 10-15 minutes, or until just warmed through.
    *   **Pan Method:** Slice thin and quickly warm in a lightly oiled hot skillet for 30-60 seconds per side. This method is excellent if you plan to use the steak in a sandwich or salad.

## Variations & Flavor Boosters

The beauty of the New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter recipe lies in its versatility. Here are some ways to customize it:

*   **Herb Power:** Experiment with different fresh herbs like sage, oregano, or even a mix. Dried herbs can work in a pinch, but fresh provides superior aroma and flavor.
*   **Spice It Up:** Add a pinch of smoked paprika, onion powder, or a dash of cayenne pepper to your seasoning mix for an extra layer of flavor.
*   **Compound Butter:** Prepare your garlic butter ahead of time by mixing softened butter with minced garlic, herbs, and other desired seasonings. Roll it into a log, chill, and then slice off discs to melt over the steak at the end of cooking.
*   **Finishing Touches:** A sprinkle of flaky sea salt just before serving can enhance the flavor and add a pleasant textural crunch. A squeeze of fresh lemon juice can brighten the richness, or a drizzle of good quality balsamic glaze can add a sweet-tangy note.
*   **Bacon Fat:** For an even richer sear, substitute some of the cooking oil with a tablespoon of rendered bacon fat.

There's truly nothing quite like the satisfaction of slicing into a perfectly cooked, juicy New York Strip Steak Garlic Butter. This American dinner recipe is a testament to how simple, quality ingredients, combined with proper technique, can yield extraordinary results. Whether you're cooking for a special occasion or simply treating yourself, mastering this dish will undoubtedly make you the hero of your kitchen. So go ahead, grab that New York Strip, and prepare to impress yourself and your loved ones with this incredible meal.
